reasoning_model_1: The audio generated by Model 1 accurately reproduces the text content '天哪，我中奖了！太不可思议了！'. Regarding the specific instruction 'extremely high pitch' (极高音), the model performs exceptionally well. The speaker (a male voice) shifts into a distinct falsetto/head voice register (0.5s - 2.8s), pushing the pitch significantly above the natural speaking range. The delivery is breathless and high-energy, perfectly capturing the 'incredible' nature of the script while strictly adhering to the high-pitch constraint. The performance sounds like someone intentionally pitching their voice up to scream in excitement.
reasoning_model_2: The audio generated by Model 2 correctly speaks the text '天哪，我中奖了！太不可思议了！'. However, regarding the 'extremely high pitch' instruction, the performance is lacking. The model uses a standard female voice with a pitch range that sounds completely normal and conversational for that voice type. While the intonation is happy, there is no distinct shift to an 'extremely high' register. It sounds like a default reading without the specific paralinguistic modification requested by the user.
model_comparison: Model 1 is the clear winner. The user requested 'extremely high pitch'. Model 1 interpreted this by generating a voice that actively pushes into a falsetto range, significantly altering the pitch to meet the 'extreme' criteria. Model 2 merely provided a standard female voice which, while naturally higher than a deep male voice, did not exhibit the requested stylistic modification to an 'extreme' level. Model 1 demonstrated control over the paralinguistic feature; Model 2 did not.
